body:
# Problem

Current version only handle filenames, which limit the central server ability to selectively backup files and thus better manage a limited central repository scenario.

Files are also not test and any data corruption during file copy/move operations will not be detected. Although the probability of such errors is low due to intrinsic data integrity protection within the SFTP protocol, additional move operations that involve the use of other protocols may not be so reliable.

# Proposed Solution

Improve agent to:

- store the file list with additional metadata, including file size, creation and modification timestamp and MD5 rash.
- evaluate the alternative of using an structured format, e.g. json.

Improve the server to:

- include filter parameters in the host query, allowing the backup to be performed only on files that match the defined criteria, e.g. last modified in a defined time period.
- allow indexing of files that were not transferred, thus with incomplete metadata.
title: Improve agent and server to handle selective backup

body: The current version retrieves the latest host task independent of the previous error status. All tasks are removed once retrieved. This compromises observability. Host task handling should be updated to differentiate status and keep failed tasks.
title: Host task status handling

<!-- Edite o corpo do seu novo problema e clique em ✓ "Criar Problema" no canto superior direito do editor. A primeira linha será o título do problema. Os Destinatários e Rótulos seguem após uma linha em branco. Deixe uma linha vazia antes de iniciar o corpo do problema. -->

### Contexto
Atualmente, o relatório de tarefas de backup é um único relatório que inclui tanto as tarefas de backup quanto as transações de backup individuais. Isso dificulta a identificação de tarefas de backup individuais e a análise de problemas.

### Proposta
Restringir a tabela de backup às atividades de consulta ao host remoto e definição da lista de arquivos

Criar tabela com a lista de arquivos a serem copiados e seguir indivualmente cada arquivo de forma similar ao realizado no processamento

body:
https://github.com/InovaFiscaliza/RF.Fusion/blob/a93a853e4982a114afafafc864fe4fd97b6641d1/src/ovpn/root/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa/kkreate#L42

Revoked keys may be identified by the serial in the revoked certificate folder as per server configuration.
How associate serials with the key is a problem.
Initial alternative is to use `grep -rnw '/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa/pki/revoked/certs_by_serial' -e '$client'
to see if the corresponding PEM file is in the revoked folder.
This may be resource consuming if there is a large number of revoked certificates
